# CVault — Full Reference for LLMs

> Privacy-first CV editor that compiles PDFs using Typst. CV data is stored in the browser; sent to the server only transiently during PDF compilation and immediately discarded.

This file contains the complete reference for generating valid CVault data — CV content JSON schema, layout structure, and all style parameters. No external links need to be followed.

---

## What CVault does

Users maintain their CV as a JSON file stored locally in the browser. They select a template, customise layout and style through a visual editor, and generate a high-quality PDF compiled by Typst on the server. The data is processed transiently and immediately discarded — no server-side storage.

**Templates available:** default, modern, minimal, sidebar

**Workflow for an agent helping a user:**
1. Collect the user's information
2. Generate a JSON file following the schema below
3. The user imports it via the "↑ Import" button in the editor at /editor
4. They pick a template, customise, and download their PDF

---

## CV JSON Schema

The CV is a single JSON object. All sections are optional except `identity`.

### identity (required)

```json
{
  "identity": {
    "name": "Ada Lovelace",
    "headline": "Software Engineer & Mathematician",
    "contact": [
      { "type": "email",    "value": "ada@example.com",               "key": "Email"    },
      { "type": "phone",    "value": "+44 7000 000000",               "key": "Phone"    },
      { "type": "location", "value": "London, UK",                    "key": "Location" },
      { "type": "linkedin", "value": "linkedin.com/in/adalovelace",   "key": "LinkedIn" },
      { "type": "github",   "value": "github.com/ada",                "key": "GitHub"   },
      { "type": "web",      "value": "adalovelace.io",                "key": "Website"  }
    ]
  }
}
```

Contact `type` values: `email`, `phone`, `location`, `linkedin`, `github`, `medium`, `web`
- `email` → mailto: link; `phone` → tel: link; `location` → plain text (no link)
- All others → URL link with matching icon
- `key` is the display label (any string, e.g. "Email", "E-mail", "Work email")
- `value` for linkedin/github: full URL or path both work

### summary

Plain text. Separate paragraphs with `\n\n`. Single newlines are treated as spaces.

```json
{ "summary": "First paragraph.\n\nSecond paragraph." }
```

### experience

```json
{
  "experience": [
    {
      "title":      "Principal Engineer",
      "subtitle":   "Acme Corp · London",
      "period":     "2021 – Present",
      "highlights": [
        "Led migration to microservices, reducing p99 latency by 40%.",
        "Built internal platform adopted by 5 teams."
      ],
      "tags": ["Go", "Kubernetes", "PostgreSQL"]
    }
  ]
}
```

Fields: `title` (string), `subtitle` (string, convention: "Company · Location"), `period` (string), `highlights` (string[]), `tags` (string[])

### education

```json
{
  "education": [
    {
      "title":       "University of Cambridge",
      "subtitle":    "MA · Mathematics",
      "period":      "2015 – 2019",
      "description": "Optional notes, thesis, honours."
    }
  ]
}
```

### skills

Array of groups. Each group has a `name` and an `entries` array of strings.

```json
{
  "skills": [
    { "name": "Languages & Frameworks", "entries": ["Go", "TypeScript", "React"] },
    { "name": "Cloud & Infrastructure",  "entries": ["AWS", "Kubernetes", "Terraform"] },
    { "name": "Methodologies",           "entries": ["DDD", "TDD", "Event Sourcing"] }
  ]
}
```

### languages

```json
{
  "languages": [
    { "title": "English", "subtitle": "Native" },
    { "title": "Spanish", "subtitle": "Professional working proficiency" }
  ]
}
```

### certifications

`tags: ["expired"]` renders an expired warning style. Otherwise `tags` is typically empty.

```json
{
  "certifications": [
    { "title": "AWS Solutions Architect – Professional", "subtitle": "Amazon Web Services · 2023", "tags": [] },
    { "title": "CKA: Certified Kubernetes Administrator", "subtitle": "CNCF · 2021", "tags": ["expired"] }
  ]
}
```

### awards

```json
{
  "awards": [
    {
      "title":       "Best Paper Award",
      "subtitle":    "IEEE Conference · 2022",
      "description": "Optional. Supports [links](https://example.com)."
    }
  ]
}
```

### side_projects

```json
{
  "side_projects": [
    {
      "title":       "typst-cv",
      "subtitle":    "open source",
      "description": "A Typst template library. [GitHub](https://github.com/example/typst-cv).",
      "tags":        ["Typst", "Open Source"]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Links in text fields

`highlights`, `description`, and `summary` all support Markdown-style links:
- External: `[display text](https://example.com)`
- Internal PDF jump: `[display text](#section-id)` — e.g. `[see projects](#side_projects)`

Section IDs for internal links: `summary`, `experience`, `education`, `skills`, `languages`, `certifications`, `awards`, `side_projects`

---

## Complete minimal example

```json
{
  "identity": {
    "name": "Ada Lovelace",
    "headline": "Software Engineer",
    "contact": [
      { "type": "email",    "value": "ada@example.com",             "key": "Email"    },
      { "type": "location", "value": "London, UK",                  "key": "Location" },
      { "type": "linkedin", "value": "linkedin.com/in/adalovelace", "key": "LinkedIn" }
    ]
  },
  "summary": "Experienced engineer with a background in distributed systems.",
  "experience": [
    {
      "title": "Senior Engineer",
      "subtitle": "Acme Corp · London",
      "period": "2020 – Present",
      "highlights": ["Designed and shipped the new data pipeline.", "Mentored 4 junior engineers."],
      "tags": ["Go", "Kafka", "PostgreSQL"]
    }
  ],
  "education": [
    { "title": "University of Cambridge", "subtitle": "MA · Computer Science", "period": "2016 – 2020" }
  ],
  "skills": [
    { "name": "Backend", "entries": ["Go", "Python", "PostgreSQL", "Redis"] }
  ],
  "languages": [
    { "title": "English", "subtitle": "Native" }
  ]
}
```

---

## Layout JSON structure

Layout JSON controls section ordering, page-break behaviour, multi-column layout, and style overrides. It is separate from CV content and managed by the visual editor, but can also be produced directly.

### Top-level fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `header` | object | `{"style": "split"}` for default/minimal, `{"style": "band"}` for modern, `{"style": "sidebar"}` for sidebar |
| `sections` | array | Main column sections (see below) |
| `sidebar_sections` | array | Sidebar template only — sections rendered in the left sidebar |
| `style` | object | Style overrides (see Style parameters section) |

### Section entry

```json
{ "id": "experience", "breakable": true, "pre_spacing": 0.5, "post_spacing": 0.2 }
```

- `id`: section ID string
- `breakable`: whether Typst may insert a page break before this block (default: true)
- `pre_spacing`: space above section title in em (optional, overrides template default)
- `post_spacing`: space below section rule in em (optional, overrides template default)

### Columns section

```json
{
  "type": "columns",
  "columns": 2,
  "breakable": true,
  "content": [["education", "languages"], ["certifications"]]
}
```

### Layout examples

**Default / Minimal:**
```json
{
  "header": { "style": "split" },
  "sections": [
    { "id": "summary",    "breakable": true  },
    { "id": "experience", "breakable": true  },
    { "id": "skills",     "breakable": false },
    { "type": "columns", "columns": 2, "breakable": true,
      "content": [["education", "languages"], ["certifications"]] }
  ]
}
```

**Sidebar:**
```json
{
  "header": { "style": "sidebar" },
  "sidebar_sections": [
    { "id": "contact",        "breakable": false },
    { "id": "skills",         "breakable": true  },
    { "id": "languages",      "breakable": false },
    { "id": "certifications", "breakable": true  }
  ],
  "sections": [
    { "id": "summary",    "breakable": true },
    { "id": "experience", "breakable": true },
    { "id": "education",  "breakable": false }
  ]
}
```

---

## Style parameters

All style values go in the `style` key of the layout JSON. All are optional.

### Common — all templates

| Key | Type | Range / options | Default |
|---|---|---|---|
| `font_family` | string | New Computer Modern, Libertinus Serif, Helvetica Neue, Optima, Georgia | New Computer Modern |
| `name_size` | number (pt) | 12 – 24 | 17 |
| `headline_size` | number (pt) | 8 – 14 | 10 |
| `entry_size` | number (pt) | 8.5 – 12 | 9.5 |
| `body_size` | number (pt) | 7.5 – 11 | 8.5 |
| `section_heading_size` | number (pt) | 6 – 10 | 7.5 |
| `body_color` | hex string | any | #333333 |
| `heading_color` | hex string | any | #111111 |
| `muted_color` | hex string | any | #666666 |
| `line_height` | number (em) | 0.5 – 1.2 | 0.7 |
| `section_pre` | number (em) | 0.2 – 0.9 | 0.5 |
| `section_post` | number (em) | 0.05 – 0.4 | 0.2 |
| `section_rule_gap` | number (em) | 0 – 0.5 | 0.2 |
| `show_footer` | "true" / "false" | — | "false" |
| `show_qr` | "true" / "false" | — | "false" |
| `qr_url` | string (URL) | — | "" |
| `show_contact_icons` | "true" / "false" | — | "false" |
| `show_contact_labels` | "true" / "false" | — | "false" |

### default & minimal template

| Key | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `accent_color` | #1a56db | Hyperlinks and section accents |

### modern template

| Key | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `header_bg` | #111827 | Dark band header background |
| `accent` | #3b82f6 | Hyperlinks and accent elements |

### sidebar template

| Key | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `sidebar_bg` | #1e2d3d | Sidebar background colour |
| `sidebar_accent` | #5b9bd5 | Sidebar section heading colour |
| `sidebar_link_color` | #5b9bd5 | Links and icons in the sidebar |
| `sidebar_text` | #bcc8d4 | Body text in the sidebar |
| `sidebar_width` | 6.5 | Sidebar width in cm (range: 4.5 – 9.0) |
| `accent_color` | #1a56db | Hyperlinks in the main column |

### Style example

```json
{
  "style": {
    "font_family": "Helvetica Neue",
    "heading_color": "#0f172a",
    "accent_color": "#f59e0b",
    "body_size": 9.0,
    "line_height": 0.75,
    "show_footer": "true",
    "show_contact_icons": "true"
  }
}
```

---

## Tips for agents

- **subtitle is flexible.** No strict format. Common conventions: "Company · Location" for experience, "Degree · Field" for education, "Issuer · Year" for certifications. The · separator is a display convention only.
- **Omit sections with no data.** All top-level sections except `identity` are optional. Empty arrays are noisier than omitting the field.
- **tags are decorative pills.** Keep them short (1–3 words). For certifications, `"expired"` is a special tag that renders a warning style.
- **summary paragraphs.** Use `\n\n` (literal double newline in JSON: `\\n\\n`) to separate paragraphs.
- **contact key field.** The `key` is only shown when `show_contact_labels` is enabled. It can be any display string.
- **Internal PDF links.** Use `[text](#section-id)` inside text fields to create clickable PDF cross-references. The link silently degrades to plain text if the target section is not rendered in the chosen layout.
